proposals.why: Committed runtime credentials create immediate operational risk and also poison future static-analysis baselines. This is a small change with outsized security value.
proposals.scope: Clinical Writer API configuration and repository hygiene. Does not change survey contracts or runtime behavior except failing fast when required configuration is missing.
validation: Run secret scanner/Skylos on services/.
Run service startup tests with missing and present settings.
Confirm rotated credentials are no longer valid in the old form.

proposals.confidence: High for locations, medium for exploitability until source flow is inspected
proposals.why: Filesystem and outbound HTTP boundaries are cross-cutting security seams. Fixing them once through shared helpers reduces repeated path/URL audit burden.
proposals.scope: Clinical Writer API monitoring/transcription retention, survey-backend report-email file handling, and survey-backend Clinical Writer client URL probing.
validation: Unit tests for path helper and URL policy.
Skylos rerun: path traversal, symlink-following, and SSRF findings should disappear or be explicitly suppressed with justification.
Manual review of all file writes under services/.

proposals.confidence: High that analyzer context is noisy; medium on exact dependency manifests until pyproject files are inspected
proposals.why: The current scan creates hundreds of dependency findings, many of which appear caused by running analysis from services/ without service-aware Python package roots. A packaging proposal converts this noise into deterministic service manifests.
proposals.scope: Python packaging metadata, uv configuration, static-analysis configuration, and dependency manifests. It should not refactor business logic.
validation: uv sync for each service/workspace.
Compile survey-backend and clinical-writer-api modules.
Rerun Skylos and confirm undeclared dependency rows collapse to actionable residuals.

proposals.confidence: Medium: findings may be false positives if router-level or middleware guards exist
proposals.why: A route-by-route auth cleanup is error-prone. A centralized authorization contract creates an auditable route matrix, clarifies public exceptions, and reduces false positives.
proposals.scope: survey-backend FastAPI route definitions, auth dependencies, tests, and route documentation. Does not change clinical-writer-api authentication unless discovered as a dependency.
validation: Route inventory test.
Unauthenticated mutation tests.
Skylos rerun with documented residual false positives.

proposals.confidence: High for complexity and size findings
proposals.why: The Clinical Writer integration client is a high-churn seam between survey-backend and clinical-writer-api. Decomposing it improves testability, isolates security policy, and prevents future integration changes from editing a 300-line function.
proposals.scope: survey-backend/app/integrations/clinical_writer/** and directly related tests. It may touch survey-worker only for shared formatter extraction.
validation: Existing clinical writer client tests pass.
New unit tests for each extracted component.
Skylos complexity for send_to_langgraph_agent is eliminated because the function no longer exists or is a small orchestrator.

proposals.confidence: High for complexity/duplication, medium for auth until route inventory is done
proposals.why: Patient and survey response routes combine routing, persistence, clinical writer invocation, report formatting, PDF/email generation, and error handling. Extracting a report-delivery service reduces duplicated risk and makes contract changes safer.
proposals.scope: survey-backend app/api/routes/patient_responses.py, survey_responses.py, integrations/email, document/PDF generation helpers, and tests.
validation: Compile survey-backend.
Route tests for patient_responses and survey_responses.
Skylos complexity reductions in create_patient_response/create_survey_response and report formatter helpers.

proposals.confidence: High for quality/architecture findings, medium on exact refactor boundaries until source is inspected
proposals.why: The Clinical Writer API implements a domain-critical AI workflow. Refactoring should preserve the 4-stage graph contract while isolating configuration, prompt resolution, model routing, monitoring, and retention policies.
proposals.scope: services/clinical-writer-api/clinical_writer_agent/** excluding generated artifacts and broad prompt content changes.
validation: Clinical Writer API contract tests.
Graph default-flow tests.
Skylos rerun with reduced architecture/complexity hotspots.

proposals.why: Without quality gates, the project will repeatedly accumulate the same typing, complexity, duplicate-code, and analyzer-noise findings. A baseline-aware gate lets you improve incrementally without blocking all work on legacy debt.
proposals.scope: Repository Python tooling and validation scripts. Does not require immediate remediation of every existing typing or complexity finding.
validation: Quality command exits nonzero for a seeded new violation.
Baseline report generated and documented.
CI/pre-commit runs on representative changed files.
